title: HST ACS/WFC Two-Axis CTE Trail Audit
question: How effectively do current ACS/WFC calibrated products suppress serial and parallel charge-transfer trails across source charge, background and transfer distance?
gap: An independent archive-product verification; not a replacement for CALACS or a new PCTETAB.
dataMode: real public HST ACS/WFC FLT/FLC products
novelty: An independent archive-product verification; not a replacement for CALACS or a new PCTETAB.
methodology: Source detection via photutils DAOStarFinder on background-subtracted FLC images. Per-source trail profiles are extracted along the parallel-readout axis (header-derived geometry, PSF-wing buffer excluded) and fit to a single empirical exponential I(d)=A*exp(-d/L)+c independently for FLT and FLC. Suppression is 1-(A_FLC*L_FLC)/(A_FLT*L_FLT). Observational uncertainty is bootstrap-resampled; numerical convergence is assessed separately via fit covariance conditioning.
assumptions: Readout-corner geometry is inferred from the CCDAMP/CCDCHIP header keywords using a documented, explicit convention (see docs/ASSUMPTIONS_AND_LIMITATIONS.md), not independently re-derived from CALACS source.
Trails are modelled as a single empirical exponential (Massey et al. 2010; Anderson & Bedin 2010 form), not a multi-trap-species physical simulation.
A 4-pixel buffer around each source excludes PSF-wing contamination before trail fitting.
limitations: First-release, bounded scope: an independent archive-product check, not a replacement for CALACS or a new PCTETAB.
The deterministic real-data sample is small by design; under-populated charge/transfer-distance bins are flagged in results/warnings.json, not hidden.
reports/report.tex could not be compiled to PDF in this environment (no local LaTeX toolchain); only structural completeness was verified.

citation.author: Biswajit Jana
citation.license: BSD-3-Clause (original code); mission/archive products retain their original terms
